source.title: The basal ganglia and chunking of action repertoires

confidence_rationale: Foundational reference establishing the chunking-of-action-repertoires construct in the striatum. Cited >2000 times. Direct mapping to ARC-071's compose-on-repetition commitment. Substrate identity (striatum) and direction (sequence -> single performance unit) both nailed. Mapping fidelity is the dominant component; the only caveat is that the 1998 paper proposes the construct rather than testing it experimentally, so source_quality is rated on conceptual rigour and subsequent empirical validation rather than primary data in the paper itself.
failure_signatures: ARC-071 must accumulate over (sequence, outcome) pairs, not over (sequence) alone -- Graybiel 1998 is explicit that S-R learning shapes the chunked unit; without outcome-keyed accumulation the slot is mis-specified.
Recoding-of-cortically-derived-information happens within the striatum, not in cortex -- ARC-071 substrate must be a striatum-analog, not a generic policy-cache.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Recoding within the striatum can chunk the representations of motor and cognitive action sequences so that they can be implemented as performance units. S-R learning has properties of slow acquisition and (in humans) can occur without conscious awareness.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-071 instantiates exactly this recoding -- repeatedly executed primitive sequences are chunked into a single primitive that downstream layers (ARC-062 rule apprehension, ARC-065 diversity generation) treat atomically. Trigger primary is repetition + outcome consistency (the canonical Graybiel pattern); substrate analog is dorsolateral striatum, sibling to MECH-163 habit system.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Graybiel 1998 frames chunking as cognitive AND motor; ARC-071 currently registers only over policy primitives (motor analog). A cognitive-chunking extension is implicit but not registered. Also, Graybiel's 'performance unit' is implementation-side; ARC-071's chunk also reshapes proposal/rule-apprehension, which is a stronger claim the 1998 paper does not explicitly support.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review synthesising rodent and primate striatal recording data and human imaging.

confidence_components.notes: Mapping fidelity dominant -- this is the canonical reference for ARC-071's architectural commitment. Transfer risk low (mammalian striatal organisation broadly conserved). Source quality 0.80 reflects review-paper-not-primary-data status.
